slides.title: You can train and still be too *sedentary*.
slides.sub: One gym hour doesn't undo nine hours in a chair. A single enzyme explains why.

slides.title: Sitting flips a switch
slides.body: Sit still and an enzyme that processes fat *switches off in your legs* within about an hour, even on a training day.

slides.title: Move every half hour
slides.body: Stand or walk *2–3 minutes* every 30 to 60 minutes. Frequent breaks beat one big workout for this.

slides.title: Smooth out the dips
slides.body: Those short movement breaks can *blunt post-meal glucose* and soften the afternoon energy crash.

slides.title: Engineer the movement in
slides.body: Take *standing calls*, walk to refill water, set a timer. Make moving the easy default, not a decision.

slides.title: Still train, just don't coast
slides.body: Keep your workouts, but *don't let them excuse* the other fifteen hours in a chair.

slides.title: Movement that *actually adds up*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps and movement through the day and feeds it into your daily score, so you see the breaks you're missing.

caption:
You can work out and still be too sedentary. One gym hour doesn't undo nine in a chair. 🪑

Sitting switches off a fat-processing enzyme in your legs within an hour. Stand or walk 2–3 minutes every half hour to blunt the glucose dips. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your steps and movement into your daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why You Can Train and Still Be Too Sedentary
